message.content: I need a store for a bitemporal knowledge graph. SQLite with two timestamp columns would work, but I'm worried about query ergonomics and not really testing the 'graph' part.

content.text: SQLite gets you row-level storage of (valid_time, transaction_time) but every graph traversal becomes a JOIN. If you want bitemporal semantics with real graph queries, look at Kuzu. It's embedded, Cypher-native, and supports temporal properties on edges out of the box.

message.content: Kuzu sounds good. What about performance? I'll be writing concepts and edges frequently during the concept extraction phase.

content.text: Kuzu's column-store backend handles heavy bulk inserts well. For write-heavy concept extraction you'd want to batch inside a transaction. The bitemporal filter at read time ((valid_from <= as_of AND valid_to IS NULL)) is a clean Cypher pattern and Kuzu indexes it efficiently.

message.content: Decision: going with Kuzu for the KG layer. I'll keep ChromaDB for vector embeddings. SQLite stays only for the legacy ConceptStore until migration is done.

content.text: That split matches how Graphiti and most temporal KG systems structure things. ChromaDB for vectors, Kuzu for the graph, ConceptStore deprecated. Keep the migration guard strict so nothing new lands in SQLite.
